metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_912
metadata.comment: Rationale: Neurabin-1 labelling mainly in spines (Fig2A and C) but also synaptic terminals (Fig 2D and E) and glia processes (Fig2G). This is quantified in Fig3 for both Neurabin-1 and Neurabin-2 (Spinophilin), both proteins are prodominantly present in the spine and also found in glia, while only Neurabin-1 is also found in synaptic terminals, not Neurabin-2 (Spinophilin). Neurobin-1 in the spine is mainly found in the PSD and the area in close proximity of the PSD (fig6 and Fig7).. Experimental description: Macaque monkey : "These experiments were performed on PFC (Walker's area 9) obtained from 9 young adult macaque monkeys" found in materials and methods.

checks.label: OWL Consistency
